However, some sources classify it as belonging to the family Asteraceae, which is a more modern classification. In either case, it is a flowering plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. It is often found growing in damp areas and has a distinctive appearance with its lobed leaves and dandelion-like flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine and as a food source in some cultures.

intro: Crucifera kernera, also known as the 'sowbane' or 'common watercress bane', is a perennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and broad, kidney-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Crucifera kernera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used as a component in ointments and salves for topical application.
